title: ConfigurationRow Object
description: Represents a row in a configuration table. The header row is not considered a standard row but is information associated with each column. For a top table, each row represents a configuration, and for a theme table, each row represents a theme.

methods.name: activate
methods.description: Causes this row to become the active row in the table.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates a new row by copying this row.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this row from the table. The first row of the top table cannot be deleted, and this method will fail.

methods.name: generate
methods.description: Causes this row to be generated.

methods.name: getCellByColumnId
methods.description: Gets the cell in this row at the column with the specified ID.

methods.name: getCellByColumnIndex
methods.description: Gets the cell in this row at the specified column index. The first column has an index of 0 and does not include the name column.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the unique ID that identifies this row. The ID remains constant for this row as long as the row exists. This is different than the name, which the user can change.

properties.name: index
properties.description: The index position of this row within the table. The first row is at index 0 and does not include the header row.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of this row. Names must be unique with respect to other rows in this table. If you specify a name that exists, Fusion will append a counter to ensure uniqueness. For example, if "Small" is already used and you name another row "Small", you will end up with "Small (1)".

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentTable
properties.description: Returns the configuration table this row is a member of.

activate.title: ConfigurationRow.activate Method
activate.description: Causes this row to become the active row in the table.
activate.return_description: Returns true if the activation was successful.

classType.title: ConfigurationRow.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: ConfigurationRow.copy Method
copy.description: Creates a new row by copying this row.
copy.return_description: Returns the newly created row or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name to use for the new row. An empty string indicates that Fusion should use the default naming scheme.   Names must be unique with respect to other rows in this table. If you specify a name that exists, Fusion will append a counter to ensure uniqueness. For example, if "Small" is already used and you name another row "Small", you will end up with "Small (1)".

deleteMe.title: ConfigurationRow.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this row from the table. The first row of the top table cannot be deleted, and this method will fail.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the deletion was successful.

generate.title: ConfigurationRow.generate Method
generate.description: Causes this row to be generated.
generate.return_description: Returns a future that can be used to determine when the generation is complete. Null is returned in the case where starting the generation fails.

getCellByColumnId.title: ConfigurationRow.getCellByColumnId Method
getCellByColumnId.description: Gets the cell in this row at the column with the specified ID.
getCellByColumnId.return_description: Returns the specified cell if successful or null if a column with the specified ID does not exist.

parameters.name: columnId
parameters.description: The ID of the column the cell is in.

getCellByColumnIndex.title: ConfigurationRow.getCellByColumnIndex Method
getCellByColumnIndex.description: Gets the cell in this row at the specified column index. The first column has an index of 0 and does not include the name column.
getCellByColumnIndex.return_description: Returns the specified cell if successful and null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: columnIndex
parameters.description: The index of the column to return the cell for. The first column has an index 0.

id.title: ConfigurationRow.id Property
id.description: Gets the unique ID that identifies this row. The ID remains constant for this row as long as the row exists. This is different than the name, which the user can change.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.id

index.title: ConfigurationRow.index Property
index.description: The index position of this row within the table. The first row is at index 0 and does not include the header row.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.index

isValid.title: ConfigurationRow.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.isValid

name.title: ConfigurationRow.name Property
name.description: Gets and sets the name of this row. Names must be unique with respect to other rows in this table. If you specify a name that exists, Fusion will append a counter to ensure uniqueness. For example, if "Small" is already used and you name another row "Small", you will end up with "Small (1)".

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
configurationRow_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: ConfigurationRow.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.objectType

parentTable.title: ConfigurationRow.parentTable Property
parentTable.description: Returns the configuration table this row is a member of.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRow_var.parentTable
